title: 2026 F1 Japanese GP RACE analysis by Peter Windsor
description: Kimi Antonelli didn't make a great start from the Suzuka pole but he recovered well before taking full advantage of a timely Safety Car on lap 22: back in front at the restart, with Oscar Piastri's McLaren-Mercedes now in his mirrors, the young Italian then disappeared into the Japanese sunset, setting fastest lap as he secured his second win in a row. Oscar duly finished a gritty P2, beating Charles Leclerc's Ferrari to the line by 1.5sec, while George Russell could finish only P4 for Mercedes

title: How Jacques Villeneuve Shook Up F1 | F1 Beyond The Grid Podcast
description:
The son of a motorsport legend, a racer since childhood, Formula 1 was Jacques Villeneuve's destiny. In 1996, the young Canadian arrived at his first Grand Prix and immediately proved he belonged there by taking pole position.

In his rookie season Villeneuve won races, went wheel-to-wheel with Michael Schumacher and took Williams teammate Damon Hill to a final race title-decider. 30 years on, he sits down with Tom Clarkson to remember how he shook up the sport.

title: 2026 F1 Chinese GP race analysis by Peter Windsor
description: New regs or not - McLaren-Mercedes in the race (or not): nothing could detract from the enormity of this day in Shanghai - from the first GP win for Mercedes' Kimi Antonelli. From the pole, he seized the race lead from Ferrari's Sir Lewis Hamilton on lap two - and was thereafter never headed.  Lewis re-applied pressure after the pit stops; and team-mate, George Russell, was up their in P2, pushing hard, as the race unfolded.  Kimi, though, was unperturbed. title: What we learned from first sprint qualifying of F1 2026
description:
Jon Noble and Edd Straw break down the first sprint qualifying of 2026 in China, featuring Red Bull's disastrous day, McLaren's improvement, Ferrari flip-flopping on its upside down rear wing, where George Russell has the upper hand on everyone (including his team-mate), how Alpine has stepped up, and why the new generation of F1 cars is looking better than it did in Australia.

00:00 Red Bull's disaster
02:00 McLaren's improvement
03:06 Ferrari drops upside down wing
04:50 Where Russell is maki

title: Chasing The Dream | 2026 Australian Grand Prix | F2 Behind The Scenes
description:
The season begins! The lights go out for the first race weekend of the year and the paddock is back in full swing. We caught up with Alex Dunne after news broke of him signing with Alpine Driver Academy, and spoke to Colton Herta as he experienced his first-ever F2 race weekend. There was drama on track as the Rodin teammates came into contact during the Feature Race in a tense start to the season. The championship fight is underway.

title: Dan Towriss: The ‘Passion & Drive’ Behind Cadillac F1 Team | F1 Beyond The Grid Podcast
description:
The newest team in Formula 1 are here to compete. Cadillac have earned the respect of their rivals. Now they’re pushing to get faster.

CEO Dan Towriss takes Tom Clarkson back to the start of the story - how he and his TWG Motorsports company first became involved in discussions about F1, conversations with General Motors and Cadillac, the work to build a team, choosing their race drivers and making their F1 debut in Australia.

Sergio Perez and Valtteri Bottas are the first Cadillac F1 drivers.

title: Formula 1 Cars, Explained with Max Verstappen
description:
These are more than cars. They’re science experiments.
If you enjoy this episode, subscribe to support more optimistic stories about tech!

I love Formula 1. This isn’t just a car race, it’s a massive group science competition. Ten teams are all fighting to be first, and on every team there are hundreds of people, spending millions of dollars, all working together to push technology to its limit. 

In this video, we’re gonna show you what it takes to build and drive some of the fastest and most
